solution:
```
class Solution:
    def findUnsortedSubarray(self, nums: List[int]) -> int:
        sorted_nums = sorted(nums)
        left, right = 0, len(nums) - 1
        while left < right and nums[left] == sorted_nums[left]:
            left += 1
        while left < right and nums[right] == sorted_nums[right]:
            right -= 1
        return right - left + 1 if right > left else 0
```
efficiency: HIGH
time complexity: O(n log n)
space complexity: O(n)
energy implications: LOW
explanation: The solution first creates a sorted copy of the input array. Then, it finds the first and last indices where the original array differs from the sorted array, which represent the boundaries of the subarray that needs to be sorted.
